questions.question: Which of the following structures is found in plant cells but NOT in animal cells?
questions.explanation: Cell walls made of cellulose are found in plant cells but are absent in animal cells. Animal cells have only a cell membrane.

questions.question: The process by which the body produces antibodies against pathogens is called
questions.explanation: Immunity is the body's ability to resist or eliminate potentially harmful foreign substances (antigens) through the production of antibodies and other immune responses.

questions.question: Which of the following hormones is produced by the adrenal gland?
questions.explanation: Adrenaline (epinephrine) is produced by the adrenal medulla (inner part of the adrenal gland). It prepares the body for 'fight or flight' responses.

questions.question: An organism that feeds on both plants and animals is called
questions.explanation: Omnivores consume both plant material (as herbivores do) and animal material (as carnivores do). Examples include humans, pigs, and bears.

questions.question: The transfer of pollen from the anther of one flower to the stigma of another flower on a different plant is called
questions.explanation: Cross-pollination (allogamy) involves transfer of pollen between flowers of different plants of the same species. It promotes genetic variation.

questions.question: Which of the following is responsible for the clotting of blood?
questions.explanation: Platelets (thrombocytes) are cell fragments that initiate the blood clotting process by releasing clotting factors and aggregating at the site of injury.

questions.question: The hormone that controls the development of secondary sexual characteristics in males is
questions.explanation: Testosterone, produced by the testes, is the primary male sex hormone. It controls development of secondary sexual characteristics such as facial hair, deepening voice, and muscle development.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of vestigial structures in humans?
questions.explanation: The appendix is a vestigial structure in humans — it is a remnant of a larger caecum used by ancestors to digest plant matter. It has little or no function in modern humans.

questions.question: The type of cell division that produces gametes is
questions.explanation: Meiosis is the type of cell division that produces gametes (eggs and sperm). It involves two divisions resulting in four haploid cells from one diploid cell.

questions.question: Which of the following diseases is caused by a virus?
questions.explanation: Measles is caused by the Measles virus (Morbillivirus). Tuberculosis is caused by bacteria, malaria by a protozoan (Plasmodium), and cholera by bacteria (Vibrio cholerae).

questions.question: The water-conducting tissue in vascular plants is called
questions.explanation: Xylem tissue conducts water and dissolved mineral salts from the roots upward to the leaves. It consists of tracheids and vessel elements that form hollow tubes.

questions.question: Which of the following is a saprophyte?
questions.explanation: Mushrooms (fungi) are saprophytes — they obtain nutrients by secreting enzymes onto dead organic matter and absorbing the digested products. This is also called saprotrophic nutrition.

questions.question: In a monohybrid cross between a tall plant (TT) and a short plant (tt), what will be the phenotype of the F1 generation?
questions.explanation: When TT is crossed with tt, all F1 offspring will be Tt (heterozygous). Since T (tall) is dominant over t (short), all F1 plants will be tall.

questions.question: Which of the following is the function of the stomata in leaves?
questions.explanation: Stomata are tiny pores on the leaf surface that allow gaseous exchange — CO₂ enters for photosynthesis and O₂ exits. They also control water vapor loss during transpiration.

questions.question: The scientific study of heredity and variation is called
questions.explanation: Genetics is the branch of biology that studies heredity (how traits are passed from parents to offspring) and variation (differences between individuals).

questions.question: Which of the following is produced during anaerobic respiration in yeast?
questions.explanation: During anaerobic respiration (fermentation) in yeast: Glucose → Ethanol + Carbon dioxide + small amount of energy. This is exploited in brewing and baking.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct order of ecological organization from smallest to largest?
questions.explanation: The hierarchy of ecological organization: Organism → Population (same species) → Community (different species) → Ecosystem (biotic + abiotic) → Biosphere (all ecosystems on Earth).

questions.question: The role of decomposers in an ecosystem is to
questions.explanation: Decomposers (fungi and bacteria) break down dead organic matter into simpler inorganic compounds, recycling nutrients back into the ecosystem.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a method of vegetative propagation?
questions.explanation: Pollination is a reproductive process involving pollen transfer, not vegetative propagation. Budding, grafting, and cuttings are all methods of vegetative (asexual) propagation.

questions.question: The condition in which an individual has three copies of a particular chromosome instead of the usual two is called
questions.explanation: Trisomy is the presence of three copies of a chromosome (2n+1). Down syndrome (trisomy 21) is a well-known example, caused by an extra chromosome 21.

questions.question: In the ABO blood group system, a person with blood group AB has
options: Antigen A only on red blood cells
Antigen B only on red blood cells
Both antigen A and B on red blood cells
No antigens on red blood cells
questions.explanation: Blood group AB individuals have both A and B antigens on their red blood cells and neither anti-A nor anti-B antibodies in plasma. They are the universal recipients.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes natural selection?
options: Organisms with favorable traits survive and reproduce more
All organisms have equal chances of survival
Humans selectively breed organisms for desired traits
Random changes in allele frequencies in a population
questions.explanation: Natural selection is the process by which organisms with favorable heritable traits (adaptations) tend to survive and reproduce more successfully, passing on their traits to offspring.

questions.question: The main function of the cuticle on a leaf is to
questions.explanation: The waxy cuticle on the leaf surface is an adaptation to reduce water loss through evaporation. It acts as a waterproof layer covering the epidermis.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of mammals?
options: They lay eggs and have scales
They are warm-blooded and suckle their young
questions.explanation: Mammals are characterized by: being endothermic (warm-blooded), having mammary glands (to nurse young with milk), hair/fur, and giving birth to live young (most species).

questions.question: The process by which plants lose water through their leaves is
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the evaporation of water from plant surfaces, mainly through stomata in the leaves. It drives the upward movement of water (transpiration stream) in the xylem.

questions.question: The deficiency of Vitamin C causes
questions.explanation: Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) deficiency causes scurvy, characterized by swollen and bleeding gums, poor wound healing, and fatigue. Vitamin C is essential for collagen synthesis.

questions.question: A population refers to
options: All organisms in a given area
All members of the same species living in the same area
All members of different species in the same area
The non-living components of an ecosystem
questions.explanation: A population is a group of individuals of the same species living in the same geographic area at the same time, who can interbreed and share a gene pool.

questions.question: Which of the following is a recessive trait in humans according to Mendelian genetics?
questions.explanation: Attached earlobes are a recessive trait. Free earlobes (F) are dominant over attached earlobes (f). An individual must be homozygous recessive (ff) to have attached earlobes.

questions.question: The function of the spleen in the human body is to
options: Filter blood and destroy old red blood cells
questions.explanation: The spleen filters blood by removing old, damaged, and abnormal red blood cells, stores platelets, and plays a role in immune responses against blood-borne pathogens.

questions.question: Which of the following adaptations helps desert plants survive in dry conditions?
options: Broad leaves to maximize water absorption
Reduced leaves and deep roots to conserve water
Large stomata to allow faster gas exchange
questions.explanation: Desert plants (xerophytes like cacti) have reduced or absent leaves (to minimize transpiration), thick waxy cuticles, sunken stomata, and deep or extensive roots to survive drought.

questions.question: The term 'biodiversity' refers to
options: The number of individuals in a population
The variety of life forms in a given area
The total biomass of an ecosystem
The rate of reproduction of organisms
questions.explanation: Biodiversity refers to the variety and variability of life on Earth, including diversity within species (genetic diversity), between species (species diversity), and of ecosystems.

questions.question: The part of the kidney where ultrafiltration occurs is the
questions.explanation: Ultrafiltration occurs in the Bowman's capsule (glomerular capsule), where high blood pressure forces water, salts, glucose, and urea from the glomerulus into the nephron tubule.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a food web component that receives energy from more than one source?
questions.explanation: Omnivores feed on both plants and animals, receiving energy from more than one trophic level. This makes them part of multiple food chains within a food web.

questions.question: During germination of seeds, the first structure to emerge from the seed is usually the
questions.explanation: The radicle (embryonic root) is the first structure to emerge during seed germination. It anchors the seedling and absorbs water and nutrients.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct definition of a dominant gene?
options: A gene that is only expressed in females
A gene whose effect is expressed even when only one copy is present
A gene that is carried on the Y chromosome
A gene that skips a generation
questions.explanation: A dominant allele is one whose phenotypic effect is expressed even when only one copy is present (in heterozygous individuals). It masks the effect of the recessive allele.

questions.question: The active transport of substances across a cell membrane requires
options: Energy in the form of ATP
questions.explanation: Active transport moves substances against their concentration gradient (from low to high concentration) and requires energy in the form of ATP, along with specific carrier proteins.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of asexual reproduction in plants?
questions.explanation: Vegetative propagation through stem tubers (e.g., potato) is asexual reproduction — new plants develop from the vegetative parts of the parent plant without fertilization.

questions.question: Which of the following carbohydrates is the storage form in plants?
questions.explanation: Starch is the storage polysaccharide in plants, found in chloroplasts and amyloplasts. Glycogen is the storage form in animals. Cellulose forms plant cell walls.

questions.question: The absorption of digested food occurs mainly in the
questions.explanation: The small intestine (particularly the ileum) is the main site of food absorption. Its wall is folded into villi and microvilli, greatly increasing the surface area for absorption.

questions.question: The theory of evolution by natural selection was proposed by
questions.explanation: Charles Darwin proposed the theory of evolution by natural selection in his 1859 book 'On the Origin of Species.' He argued that favorable inherited traits increase survival and reproductive success.

questions.question: Which of the following is the primary function of the urethra?
options: Carry urine from the bladder to the outside
questions.explanation: The urethra is the tube that carries urine from the urinary bladder to the outside of the body during urination. In males, it also carries semen.

questions.question: Which nitrogen base is found in RNA but NOT in DNA?
questions.explanation: Uracil is found in RNA and pairs with Adenine. In DNA, Thymine plays the same role and pairs with Adenine. RNA contains Adenine, Guanine, Cytosine, and Uracil.

questions.question: The hardening of arteries due to deposits of fat is called
questions.explanation: Atherosclerosis is the buildup of plaques (fatty deposits, including cholesterol) within arterial walls, causing hardening and narrowing of arteries and increasing cardiovascular disease risk.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a niche?
options: The geographical location of an organism
The role and position of an organism in its ecosystem
The habitat where an organism lives
questions.explanation: An ecological niche describes the role an organism plays in its ecosystem, including its habitat, feeding habits, interactions, and all biotic and abiotic conditions it requires.

questions.question: In which of the following groups are organisms that lack a cell nucleus?
questions.explanation: Bacteria are prokaryotes — they lack a membrane-bound nucleus. Their genetic material (DNA) is located in the nucleoid region of the cytoplasm. All other options are eukaryotes.

questions.question: The pigment responsible for the red color in ripe tomatoes is
questions.explanation: Lycopene is the carotenoid pigment responsible for the red color in ripe tomatoes and watermelons. It is also a powerful antioxidant.

questions.question: Parthenogenesis is defined as
options: Development of a new organism from an unfertilized egg
Development of an organism from a fertilized egg
questions.explanation: Parthenogenesis is the development of an organism from an unfertilized egg. It occurs naturally in bees, wasps, and some reptiles, and produces haploid or diploid offspring.

questions.question: Which of the following is the function of tendons?
questions.explanation: Tendons are tough connective tissue bands that connect muscles to bones, allowing muscles to move the skeleton when they contract. Ligaments connect bone to bone.

questions.question: The process by which organisms of the same species compete for the same limited resources is called
questions.explanation: Intraspecific competition occurs between individuals of the same species competing for the same limited resources (food, mates, territory). It is often intense because needs are identical.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct function of the pancreas?
questions.explanation: The pancreas has two functions: endocrine (producing insulin and glucagon to regulate blood sugar) and exocrine (producing digestive enzymes like amylase, lipase, and proteases).
